name: ILifecycleHook
description: Contract for workflow lifecycle hooks. Hooks receive notifications at key points during workflow execution. All methods have default no-op implementations — subclasses override only the callbacks they need.

methods.name: on_workflow_start
methods.description: Called when a workflow execution begins.

methods.name: on_workflow_end
methods.description: Called when a workflow execution completes successfully.

methods.name: on_step_start
methods.description: Called before a step begins execution.

methods.name: on_step_end
methods.description: Called after a step completes successfully.

methods.name: on_error
methods.description: Called when a step encounters an error.

methods.name: on_retry
methods.description: Called when a step retry is attempted.

methods.name: on_decision
methods.description: Called when the executor records a decision. Receives a Decision dataclass capturing intent, options, chosen option, and reasoning.

methods.name: on_budget_threshold
methods.description: Called when cumulative cost reaches the degradation threshold.

methods.name: on_approval_requested
methods.description: Called when an approval request is submitted for a workflow step. Fires when the executor determines that a step requires human approval based on the configured risk policy.

methods.name: on_approval_received
methods.description: Called when an approval decision is received for a workflow step. Fires after the approval gate returns a result.

behavioral_expectations: All methods have default no-op implementations — subclasses override only the callbacks they need
ILifecycleHook() is a valid null-object instance (all methods are no-ops by default)
Implementations must not raise exceptions from lifecycle callbacks — errors should be logged internally
Lifecycle callbacks are async and must be non-blocking
ApprovalResult contains: request_id (str), status (ApprovalStatus), approver (str | None), timestamp (float), metadata (dict)
